Oh, it's online, right about when is the best time to ask for referrals and The collective wisdom out there among the so-called sales traders right for all that, right? We'll know what they're about most of them right people told them They say you have to wait the problem the mistake that people make is they ask for referrals too quickly too early in the game and Because that they blow the whole thing up and I just gotta tell you something guys. I've sold everything I have a lot of financial problems household goods food entertainment tangible intangible online, you know, you know, I asked referrals Right as soon as I close someone I asked them immediately and I start saying them and I get them I get more referrals of anyone. I know in fact when I was in the mortgage business I got so many referrals. It was like almost legendary How many I get from like one person right and there's a reason why and I'll tell you what it is See when you're using the straight line what happens when I close I Don't close someone by the skin of my teeth It's not like they're at a low level of certainty and I just impulse them into a sale You get it knows when I close someone I've gotten them to such a high level of certainty both logically and emotionally and I've also Positioned myself from the very start as this world-class expert high-level expert and because of that I've essentially earned the right or put another way they feel comfortable enough to give me referrals right away I don't have to wait for some arbitrary amount of time to pass until they've seen all the benefits experience them They've you know got the whole thing under their belts. They just know for sure, right? They're so certain When I close That they run to give me referrals and I'm not saying this to impress you about my ability something to do with that This is about you understanding that now that you're really closing and you know you're perfecting the straight line system you Close people not in the traditional way where they're just in pulse that barely close You really got a solid person on your hands and that person is gonna give you a furl straight away Now not every single time, but I would say you know 50 60 percent of the time you ask so for a person I'll tell you this way about this 95 percent of the time someone who's ultimately gonna give you a referral will give it to you right away Some people just don't give referrals out right but this notion that you have to wait for three months or six months That is not going to work And let me just tell you what ends up happening in the same way that you have like you know in love between men and women You have the friend zone and you got the love zone right? Either your friends or your intimate right? Well when you spend too much time in the friend zone You never can get back into the love zone. You're an established friend zone person right well when it comes to referrals You have the referral zone and you're the non-referral zone You're one of those books that they don't give referrals to or they do give a personal if you spend too much time with someone and you're Been there provider of a service or a good and you haven't gotten referrals You're never gonna get any you get into that zone of zone. You know how there's the Macy's Santa and you know, he's the real Santa of course in the movie, right? And he Referrs some woman to gimbals and back in the 30s or 40s whatever it was right and because gimbals was Macy's competitor And the woman comes in to buy ice-gates for a kid They're under the right type of ice-gates and the Santa Claus sends the woman to the competitor And of course the upper level executives find out and they want to fire the Santa Claus But it turns out that the newspapers get a hold of snake turn it into this massive PR storm right and mr. Macy himself realizes the value of this and Of course then mr. Gimbal finds out wait a second Macy's is doing I want to send my customers to Macy's and then mr. Macy mr. Gimbal like shake hands is alright howling expert, right? But the point is is that when you're on the receiving end of it? It is profound. Let me tell you something Brie only could not have sold me a suit that day. They didn't have it in stock, right? My next clothing purchase is gonna be from Brie Odie. I Feel indebted to them. I feel that I owe them something that's reciprocity and they have beautiful stuff right there And famous you know for having the greatest suits, right? You know, I'm gonna make it a point Now I'm not gonna run there with a marker But next time I'm walking down Madison Avenue or be a rodeo drive if I pass by a brioche So you're damn sure I'm gonna go in there and I'm gonna try to buy something because of the level of Customer service that they gave me now that in this case, you know, I've been a customer So I guess I'm already a customer of rione. I've put a lot of their clothes in the past, right? I never a suit so I'm already a customer and they refer me out. So like that's what we call Creating a customer for life. That's one of the rules I teach It's so powerful why well number one obviously there's the reciprocity, right? They do this for you I'm like wow. I'm just blown away, but also I Know that in the future if I need some type of clothing and you know, maybe there's a brioche nearby If I walk into brioche if that's their policy, that's how they treat their good customers And I know even if they don't have what I need Guess what the sales person will send me to where I got to go So I'm always gonna look at brioche now as a first point of contact It's so powerful and I'm glad that I get to endorse brioche right now All right because of what they did they just treated me like gold they send me to that competitor Now obviously, let me say this there are certain industries with this just would not make any sense, right? So if you're in a business, we have like one product and it's like, you know, if they were to use your competitor They'll never use you and obviously you don't want to send that customer to that competitor But that's more the exception to the rule for the most part you can send people to your competitor and In nurse such good will as a result of that That you truly then have a customer for life that will give you referrals I'm gonna refer people to brilliant. I'm doing it right now I feel I just think I feel I feel warm and fuzzy about them and they got beautiful clothes and everyone knows that that was just my own experience so When I teach this whole notion of creating customers for life I have this set of guidelines of protocols or a six or seven of them in all right and let me give you a couple of them right now Then I want to move into some other aspects of you know, what you do after you close a sale How do you take this one-time buyer and ensure that they do become a long-term customer that? buys again and again and gives you referrals, right? So one of the main things here one of the overarching rules ready for Don't ever duck a phone call or an email or a text in other words If there's a problem you sell something no matter how hard you try there's gonna be problems, right? If a customer is trying to solve a problem and you make yourself scarce You duck a phone call in the olden days That's what used to be you know very easy to do now. It's the texting is not quite easy But someone texts you don't respond. You just make yourself scarce You will never fully have this customer's trust again Can the relationship be sort of prepared? Yeah, somewhat but never fully the most toxic thing that you can do is Duck someone's call when there's a problem. Here's the deal. We are conditioned We've been essentially desensitized to expect and accept bad news We know that when we buy things it don't always go as planned We know things are gonna break they're not gonna function well But the question is what do you do when that happens? What do you do when you're on the ass end of that situation? You know you sell something and whatever and it just there's a problem Do you be the person that puts your best foot forward and helps that customer resolve that problem? What do you hide behind your telephone or your your smartphone with texting on what's up? You don't answer you make yourself scarce right? Let me tell you something Stratton with all the mistakes I made with ethics and integrity back then with customer service one thing I never allowed we did not allow our sales people to duck phone calls never Not always is the death knell of any long-term business But you're only begging for someone to sue you what at least to complain and nowadays with the internet come on your reputation Is everything it's really hard to repair So you got to get out in front and here's the irony by the way is that once there's a problem And you actually resolve it It's an opportunity to build even more trust Think about when you're on the receiving end of that what happens when you buy something Think back and it didn't actually function or work the way it was supposed to the problem Something went wrong and the sales person who sold a few steps old you stepped in Went above and beyond to make sure that your problem got sorted out Rather just passing it off to a customer service person or simply being scarce, which is the worst thing right? What did you feel inside about the salesperson? How did it impact your perception? Did you want to buy from this person again? Did you what did you resent them for even having a problem in the front? No You wouldn't resent to say oh man. I didn't problem. No you'd say wow This company they stand behind their word. What are the great opportunities to build trust? To develop a long-term customer for life is precisely that When problems arise you want to handle them immediately and don't just pass them off to someone else Don't say oh, yeah, go to my yeah, you know go to the customer success or customer service. Don't do that Get be a mention they say right get on the phone as much as at least at least Put transfer the person be involved in the process even if you can't fix it yourself keep your finger in the process I promise you the level of trust and appreciation That that brings into the relationship is priceless truly. Okay now next big one We always talk about this obvious fact that you want to this is an obvious one, right? But I'm gonna take it a different way you want to Under promise and over deliver now that's so obvious that it's almost a cliche everyone says it Very few people actually do it, but it's a cliche We all know that you're supposed to under promise and over deliver right, you know We call this the management of expectation The worst thing you could do is you know is you'll make all these wild Promise to someone and then you deliver so much like you're all go above and beyond but Even you're above and beyond is not living up to the outrageous promises you made and they hate you This is not about the promise and what you deliver. It's about the management of Expectation like it's like an arbitrary thing. What does the product do? What is it? What benefits does it deliver what features does it have right? You need to not go on and on So here's where I want to dig in. This is the difference Why does it happen if we all know how toxic it is? To over promise and I'm gonna why there's so many sales people do it, you know why because they don't Write out or don't even know about looping patterns What happens is as the sales starts to drag on and they run out of things to say they start making things up They start exaggerating making claims and building and building on the on there on the benefits until they've ended up in a spot That's outrageous. It's not grounded in reality So now even if the person closes and they have let's say Product works exactly as it's been designed to work But you said things in the process of closing and you're looping That made this thing seem like it was like the next, you know, who's like the Messiah itself. You're done. I Don't care how great the benefits were if you're all promised Yeah, you're not gonna have a good end here. This is about the management of expectation So what's the solution? Well, what I want you to do is I want you to really take a good look at all of the looping Patterns that you've developed for each of the three tens remember you have a primary secondary tertiary You know the fourth one as well, right make sure That in the heat of the moment when you were right never really mentioned Reality check here make sure that in the process of closing You don't say things that put you into that zone where you can't possibly deliver It's the stupidest thing you could do and I'll tell you 99% of the time It's simply because the salesman didn't take the time to prepare They didn't have enough intelligent things scripted out to say and because of that they said something that was outrageous When by the way, they could have said something that was totally in line with what they could deliver on and they blow it What a shame? The management of expectation is everything So please please please I want you to as a reality check here And this is by the way in the the certification train We go back now in this exercise portion I take you through all of that and we have detailed examples and show you how to act If not, you're on that you got to go back and check these patterns It's the death knell of a long-term relationship and also you're gonna get cancellations. All right, so be mindful of that. All right next Here's an interesting one about giving gifts There is a couple of places we and I'm talking about not major gifts I'm talking about ethical gifts. They could be as small as a thank-you note It could be a coffee table book with pictures of you know famous locations or travel destinations or food or restaurants I depending on what we call the prospect or your new customers aliveness meaning what makes them feel alive outside of work Put it this way the worst gift you can give a plumber is what a wrench You don't give gifts to people that are essentially things that they would use in the workplace, you know You're not gonna give a second. Oh, here's a free stapler That's not a gift that's gonna inner any good will towards you You want to find out what their interests are outside of work now? You say wait a second Jordan. I thought that that's going off the Pluto. Yes, it is when you're in that Pre-closing phase so when you're gathering intelligence before you've closed someone Absolutely, you don't want to be spending all this time on their personal life That's the telltale side of a novice and it does not bill report actually repels people but Once you close someone Guess what the rules of the game change now you actually want to go off the blue it's not blue anymore Pluto actually comes inside the boundaries and Questions about their personal life interests birthdays and adversaries. What are their children do? How old are they? You know, do they go to college with finding out about what someone's? Life is like outside of work is crucial because then when you give gifts to people You want to meet those needs tailor your gifts to match what people feel? Alive about outside of work. Let me give you another great example here back in the day right when I had stratton We had this massive phone bill so every single phone company and was in the early days of deregulation Right there were all after me for my half a million dollar a month phone bill, right? One day company comes along and says are they and actually had they were providing with some line some service, right? They gave me tickets to like I was like a playoff game for baseball the Mets or something or the Yankees And you know what I hate baseball. I Couldn't care less. I almost felt offended like they were trying to bribe me seriously I felt like okay. Well, they're giving me tickets. I wonder what's wrong with their products I mean, I really was not impressed that I was turned off Yet now I know Roger Federer and the dollar only probably eight or nine years old at the time, right? But if they would have given me tickets to the US Open what a Wimbledon what Augusta forgot man They would have had my business The person made the fatal mistake of not finding out what I liked guys. This is so profound It's so easy to do so not it's so easy to follow us not make this mistake But before you go out giving gifts to people don't invite people to sporting events or into skyboxes If they don't like what you're offering I still get it all a lot of people inviting me out to places that I don't care about and no interest at all It tells me is that trying to bribe me their progress probably subpar because they need to bribe me versus If someone takes the time to find out what I really like and then fills that need That's how you develop a customer for life that's how you develop reciprocity and it builds massive trust massive rapport and Set you up for greatness. I promise you that Onboarding is crucial and then the next phase is called adoption and what that means now it differs from onboarding And since onboarding is at the early stage people get basic familiarity with your project start using it Adoption means when it's us to get rolled out to the entire company to be the situation Or when someone has to learn the site using all the various and the deeper features secondary features and benefits in other words They really get in and start using it that's maximum potential across the board You must not end the sale here the sale begins here now You got a onboard adoption and then it's in this adoption phase and by the way right in your onboarding Right after your onboarding in your adoption where you start to engage in upsells for ourselves and You ask for referrals right here in your onboarding Okay, and I would get to that in a separate name. Perfecting the straight line in terms of closing it But you're for sure right here And you don't make sure that people have a great experience and They start actually not just using it a little bit But use it in earnest use the whole product and get the most out of that Ethical it's great business at least massive referrals and people that buy from you again and again now Before I open this up to questions here, how long we have how long has it been so far? 40 yeah Okay, and In terms of referrals on the hand of referrals Fully in a separate train here, right? But I want to just I want to give you some food for thought it before I open this up the questions I wonder a lot of Q&A today, right? So I'll go as long as you want. I just keep asking me to ask me questions right? When it comes to referrals The key here is language How you ask them you never want to say oh can you do it you can can you give me some referrals? You also when you can refer to me the word referral has a lot of you know Not really rightfully so but that doesn't just is some negative baggage attached as money being made Possibly kick it's just like it's not the right word to use so we don't call referrals It says you anyone who I can help in the same way that I helped you and It's about helping someone You know anybody like you knew help has the same problem or simple problem I could help in the same way help you and now that's sort of phase one so that you're helping With your service right we brought us solving a problem The second part is the most important one of all There's two types of ways to Ask someone a question and get a response one way is called fill in the blank Fill in the blank means do you know anyone who you who I could help in the same way that I helped you? Fill in that to fill in the blank Far more effective is to use what's called multiple choice meaning give them prompts and Friends and family and neighbors and business associates anyone can think of role you give them the answers That you're looking for those tell them Exactly a what criteria you're looking for if you do that it makes people feel much more comfortable To our first they know they won't grace in your time or the person's time people will scare it They don't know if I like that person to be right. What if they hope is that it's just a waste of time They don't waste their friends time So the more specific you know anyone that could help that maybe is you know I'm behind on their credit cards or someone that is having the same problems You would have yourself so many things out there, right? But the point is in any friends any neighbors in the area any co-workers and your businesses And you can think of it all you play multiple choice. I I promise you this test it out you'll get 500% I'm serious more referrals by Telling them who you want specifically what type people and then giving them prompts Friends family neighbors business associates Other people it's a beat-a-bees and other people that are in the business like you're similar business anyone you could think of Listen, oh, yeah, you're jogging their memory It's so much more effective than just saying you know anybody who I could help Suffering for the same problem. You know whatever might be right. I can help in the same way I helped you You're gonna get so much of a better response by playing the multiple choice game All right, and use word I can help. Okay. You like serve in the same way Language is powerful if you use the wrong words. I used to get referrals All right now one more point about referrals before we opens up to questions and answers and tell both of them up, okay? So, um, here's the deal Like anything else We always work backwards the straight line is a backwards-looking system What I mean by that is that if I want to Build a referral business. Well the first question I asked myself And I'm by the way, I live this as a salesperson. I always do how many referrals do I want to get each month? What's my target? You have to set a target Without that you have nothing to aim for it always thoughts with having a target to aim for a goal That's my goal. How many referrals do I want to get each month? Now I'll give you a perfect example when I was in the mortgage business, right? I used to go out into the field. We also did cold calling we had marketing as well But I personally I love going out door to door, right? I love going business to business and I was making so much money Calling business to business and say hey, um, you're the owner around it. Say, I am Hey, hey, my name's Jordan. I'm a mortgage broker. Hey, what rate you paying right now on your on your home? That was as simple as that no magic words and they say I'm paying eight and a half my goal Really? I can definitely save you a ton of money. I take out my book, right and start doing my thing, right? Well, here's the deal. I was going to stores for the most part that were like Neighborhood seafood store the owner's there, right? I go into Hasselons I go into any business you can imagine a You know one of the mailbox stores, right? Anything in every restaurant didn't matter. I went to a jewelry store You name it with auto body shops every single one But also I was keenly aware that there were certain types of people That could give me massive referrals for instance accountants tax planners tax services real estate Asians brokers, right? Lawyers estuary, right? So I made it a point. I said every single day No matter how many doors I've hit no matter how many people I've closed I am going to make it a point to approach at least 10 Lawyers slash accountants slash I had ten a day I would hit if I had a drive out of my way just to hit ten of these every single day I did it. I would not go home until I did and I ended up hitting People that became such massive referrals of business one example was a place called it was called Farron tax nice people the world out in the land, right and I walked in there They were incredibly hostile to me, right? You know, I walked into the whole thing and then within five minutes You know, she's like, I'll give you one, right? Because I did my thing with the straight line, right? These people became my best source. They probably gave me a hundred and fifty referrals in one year I made an average of eight to ten thousand each one so for a million dollars one door Every single day I made it a point to knock on at least ten doors that were in the referral zone Even if I had to set the clock at night, I'm out there. I'm just I'm not going home So it starts with setting a target to aim for and then working backwards How many do you have to hit each day? Why was it ten?
